inline_comments.message: should I add the contents? URL Encoded yaml policy? I hate the URL encode but I don't think we have a choice.

I know we have to cope with whatever we get but for well formed TOSCA, this should be:

For well formed TOSCA, policies should be in the topology template:
tosca_definitions_version: tosca_simple_yaml_1_0_0
topology_template:
  policies:
 
<blah blah>
The rest of the definition looks fine so I'll start with this one.
Ok no problem - I'll fix these.
wait are you sure about that? topology_template has to be there

The policy-id should be an unique identifier.  Per wiki API spec this is the base URL for Operational Policies:

https:{url}:{port}/policy/api/v1/policytypes/onap.policies.controlloop.Operational/versions/{versions}/policies

We may have 2 types of URLs to create an Operational Policy (or may be just support one) which would be a POST and a PUT one:

POST
https:{url}:{port}/policy/api/v1/policytypes/onap.policies.controlloop.Operational/versions/{versions}/policies

In this case Policy will generate the Policy ID which I think we are saying it will be the controlLoopName in the example structure.

The other option is to do a PUT and in this case CLAMP will generate the policy-id and give it to us.   Ideally the controlLoopName would be chosen provided it would give it an unique identity within CLAMP and policy.  The URL in this case:

PUT
https:{url}:{port}/policy/api/v1/policytypes/onap.policies.controlloop.Operational/versions/{versions}/policies/ControlLoop-vCPE-48f0c2c3-a172-4192-9ae3-052274181b6e

A couple notes:

1. For Operational policies, we said that CLAMP is agnostic to policy-types, so the "onap.policies.controlloop.Operational" is fixed in the URL for these types of policies.

2. Ideally the policy-id and the version would be kept within the policy itself for debugging and for human consumption.   If we choose the controlLoopName identifier as the policy-id is already there.   For version, I don't think the one insider the controlLoop was created for this purpose.

With regard to the above message in json, I don't think it needs to be jsonized with the URL encoding which is a bit of a kludge.   I think the Operational Policy can be placed as is in the body with the Content-Type plain/text or yaml and we should be able to read it in.   In some internals APIs for testing I just used the text Content-Type which goes in a String in jax-rs and feed it to the snakeyaml parser to get the object, really simple.   Anything to move away of the URL encoding is a step forward.
I don't see any value on echoing back the policy CLAMP sent.   I don't think it should be sent back.

I think we can't indicate @Consumes({"application/json", "application/yaml", "application/json; vnd.onap.guard", "application/yaml; vnd.onap.operational"}) all together for one endpoint as otherwise what would be the class I should put here. This class will be rendered in swagger ui for payload example. Our client would use different non-tosca payload for guard and ops policy. So can I separate different content-types into different endpoints, each support one content-type? I mean for guard and ops custom content-type.
No this API should have those headers. You need a different class than ToscaPolicy then. I thought Liam made a suggestion for that.

This is the only part I don't know how to do yet - supporting different request/response models based on headers like content-type in a single API endpoint
Similar issue was posted https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification/issues/146

One solution is to use path/method overloading (multiple identical paths) to achieve association of a content-type with a request/response model. If we can't do this, don't know how to do it.
Per Liam you are using the wrong structure here.
I have changed it to ToscaServiceTemplate in hotfix review that has been merged.
